creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Thu, Mar 06, 2014 at 02:43:00PM +0200, Vasil Dimov wrote:
> --- /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk.orig    2014-03-06 14:38:26.000000000 +0200
> +++ /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk    2014-03-06 14:38:15.000000000 +0200
> @@ -5553,7 +5553,7 @@
>  .endif
>  .endfor
>  .endif
> -    @if [ -f ${PLIST} ]; then \
> +    @if [ -f "${PLIST}" ]; then \
>          ${SED} ${PLIST_SUB:S/$/!g/:S/^/ -e s!%%/:S/=/%%!/} ${PLIST}
> >> ${TMPPLIST}; \
>      fi

There are, unfortunately, more than one case of blind trust that externally
coming variable values are non-empty.  Quick grep for e.g. 'if \[' | grep
-v \" can give you the picture.  Ideally, all of them should be reviewed,
and if quotes are indeed missing (unless it's guaranteed that variable in
question cannot be empty), then they should be added.

> but maybe it should be fixed in the ports themselves too?

Most likely; lots of ports are doing evil things that can trigger sublte
bugs in bsd.*.mk code.  They should also be fixed, of course.

> There are these two other ports that set PLIST to an "interesting" value:
> 
> /usr/ports/security/sinfp/Makefile:PLIST=        ""
> /usr/ports/net-p2p/libtorrent-rasterbar-python/Makefile:PLIST=
> ${NONEXISTENT}

./danfe
